1.question: With what feeling does Paul say he makes request for them in his prayers?

1.title: Who begins the good work
1.text: Paul is confident that he who has begun a good work in the saints will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ, teaching that spiritual growth is a work God brings to completion.

1.template: And this I pray, that your {0} may abound yet more and more in knowledge

1.title: The palace guard
1.text: Paul says his bonds in Christ became manifest in all the palace, the imperial praetorian guard of Rome, showing that even in captivity his witness reached the heart of the empire.

1.question: According to Paul, what effect did his bonds have on many of the brethren?
options: They waxed confident and were bold to speak the word without fear
They fell silent out of fear

pairs.right: Wrong motive of some who preached
Right motive of others who preached

1.title: Magnified by life or by death
1.text: Paul hopes Christ will be magnified in his body "whether it be by life, or by death," expressing the peace of one who entrusts the outcome of his own life to the Lord.

1.template: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is {0}.

1.question: Why does Paul judge it more needful to remain with the saints rather than depart?
options: For their sake, for their furtherance and joy of faith
Because he had not yet preached the gospel

pairs.right: How the saints should stand together
To them an evident token of perdition
To them a token of salvation, and that of God

2.question: According to verse 3, how should each person esteem others?

2.title: The hymn to Christ
2.text: Verses 5 through 11 are regarded by many scholars as one of the earliest Christian hymns, describing the humbling and exaltation of Christ. It is one of the most celebrated passages on the divine nature of Jesus.

2.template: But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a {0}, and was made in the likeness of men:

pairs.right: Christ took the form of a servant
Became obedient unto the death of the cross
Gave him a name above every name

2.question: How does Paul say we should work out our own salvation?

2.template: Do all things without {0} and disputings:

2.question: Whom did Paul trust to send shortly to the Philippians?

2.title: Who was Epaphroditus
2.text: Epaphroditus was a member of the church at Philippi sent to help Paul in prison. He fell sick nigh unto death while serving, and Paul sent him back with praise, asking that he be received with honor.

2.question: What happened to Epaphroditus while serving?
options: He was sick, nigh unto death

2.template: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all {0}; and hold such in reputation:

3.title: Dogs and circumcision
3.text: By calling those who insisted on circumcision "dogs," Paul turns around an insult some Jews applied to Gentiles, warning the saints not to trust in outward rites.

3.question: By his lineage, which tribe of Israel did Paul say he descended from?

3.template: and do count them but {0}, that I may win Christ

pairs.right: and the power of his resurrection

3.template: I press toward the {0} for the prize of the high calling of God

3.question: Where does Paul say our conversation (citizenship) is?

4.question: What does Paul tell the Philippians to do always in the Lord?

4.title: The peace that passes understanding
4.text: Paul writes of a peace that 'passeth all understanding' while he himself is a prisoner in Rome, showing this peace does not depend on outward circumstances.

4.template: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and {0} with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.

pairs.left: Whatsoever things are of good report

4.template: I can do all things through {0} which strengtheneth me.

4.question: From whom did Paul receive the things the Philippians sent?
According to what will God supply all the Philippians' need?
options: According to his riches in glory
According to the law of Moses
